mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer demand momentum, which drives corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following positive RSAFS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor demand that precedes negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and deceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicate a positive supply shock that expands corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 55% of months where INDPRO increases and CPIAUCSL decreases jointly.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is below 0.5%, a month-over-month increase in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals rising inflation that erodes real equity returns through compressed risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not negative in > 50% of low-rate high-inflation months, or mean return is not statistically below zero (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which leads to positive operating leverage and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Directional accuracy of INDPRO month-over-month sign predicting positive 5d returns falls below 52% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate exceeds 2.5%, a further increase in FEDFUNDS tightens financial conditions and raises discount rates, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods (FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and rising), SPX 60d forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirm broad-based demand expansion that reduces recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase, SPX 5d forward returns are not positive in > 58% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 50 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment that drags down cyclical equity exposure, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns following a >50k drop in HOUST are negative in fewer than 45% of instances.
